g.text: WRITE ORDERING: Why fsync Position Matters
Torn Page: Single pwrite Is NOT Atomic
Crash at ANY step: journal holds originals — recovery always possible
Journal is empty. DB has partial new data. CANNOT RECOVER.
4096-byte B-tree Leaf Page After Partial Write
0x0000-0x01FF | sector 1 | NEW data — written before power cut
0x0200-0x03FF | sector 2 | NEW data — written before power cut
0x0400-0x07FF | sector 3 | OLD data — power cut mid-write
Header says 6 cells but cell content area is half stale
4KB pwrite spans 8 x 512-byte disk sectors — not one atomic op
MUST | journal write | pwrite(jfd, orig_page) | stores undo data
MUST | journal fsync | fsync(jfd) | DURABILITY BARRIER
SAFE | db write | pwrite(dbfd, new_page) | only after r2
SAFE | db fsync | fsync(dbfd) | data persistent
COMMIT| journal del | unlink(journal_path) | tx is permanent
Swapping r3 before r2: next crash yields no undo data — corruption is permanent
